weapon.scale_note: Confirm first-person and world scale against the owning weapon prefab.
reference_requirements: Collect front, side, top, and close-up reference for the intended weapon family or equipment role.
Call out real material zones, wear patterns, decals, labels, lights, and moving parts before modeling.
Record first-person and third-person readability goals so viewmodel polish does not break world pickup clarity.
quality_targets: Silhouette reads clearly in first person and as a world-held item.
Materials separate metal, polymer, rubber, glass, tape, lights, and dirt without collapsing into flat grey.
Edges use believable bevels and weighted normals instead of razor-sharp blockout geometry.
Sockets, muzzle/effect origins, and grip orientation are authored as visible named helpers or empties.
visual_review_checks: Render a Blender preview from first-person, side, and three-quarter angles before export.
After import, inspect the owning prefab in S&Box for scale, pose, material remaps, and socket placement.
For alpha, emissive, glass, or decals, include a texture/contact-sheet or editor screenshot proof.
acceptance_checks: Muzzle or effect origin is documented when the asset fires or emits effects.
Grip and attachment orientation are documented for soldier mounting.
Standalone prefab target is recorded for attachable equipment.

drone.scale_note: Confirm visual bounds against DroneController prefab scale and flight readability.
reference_requirements: Collect reference for the exact drone class, arm geometry, motor layout, camera pod, antenna layout, and battery/body shell.
Document variant-specific cues for GPS, FPV, and fiber FPV so gameplay identity is visible without UI.
Record expected gameplay viewing distances for airborne silhouette, ground pickup, and chase-camera framing.
quality_targets: Silhouette stays readable at gameplay distance and does not disappear into the map background.
Frame, motor, propeller, lens, antenna, battery, and fastener materials are visually distinct.
Propeller pivots, camera orientation, and antenna/fiber attachments are authored as stable named children or helpers.
Bounds and origin support prefab placement, camera follow, crash behavior, and collision authoring.
visual_review_checks: Render Blender previews from top, front, side, and chase-camera angles.
After import, inspect the drone prefab with propeller children, scale, material remaps, and camera framing visible.
For transparent props, lights, decals, or fiber cables, include contact-sheet or editor screenshot proof.
acceptance_checks: Silhouette is readable from gameplay distance.
Variant identity is distinct for GPS, FPV, or fiber FPV.
Collision and gameplay components remain owned by prefab/code.

character.scale_note: Confirm proportions against existing GroundPlayerController and first-person visibility assumptions.
reference_requirements: Collect full-body, gear, material, and class/team reference before changing character-facing assets.
Document skeleton, rig, attachment, material-group, and first-person visibility assumptions.
Record how the asset should look under Soldier, Pilot, and training-dummy presentation rules.
quality_targets: Proportions, gear breakup, and team/class cues read in third person without relying only on HUD text.
Skin, cloth, armor, straps, gloves, lenses, and gear use separate believable material responses.
Attachment sockets and animation/rig assumptions are explicit before prefab wiring.
First-person arms or held-item visibility remains consistent with the real human-body/IK path.
visual_review_checks: Render front, side, back, and gameplay-distance Blender previews before export.
After import, inspect the owning character prefab in S&Box with animation helper, material groups, and held-item pose visible.
For cloth/cards/hair/transparency, include texture/contact-sheet or editor screenshot proof.
acceptance_checks: Rig, armature, or attachment readiness is documented when animation is expected.
Class or team material groups are documented when relevant.
Existing SoldierBase prefab identities remain unchanged.

environment.scale_note: Confirm origin and dimensions are sensible for scene placement.
reference_requirements: Collect scale, construction, material, wear, and gameplay-cover reference before detailed modeling.
Document intended placement context, repetition count, collision expectations, and player traversal impact.
Record material breakup for surface, trim, glass, metal, dirt, foliage, decals, and damage states when relevant.
quality_targets: Large forms read from drone height while close surfaces hold up for soldier-scale inspection.
Materials avoid one-note color fills and include believable roughness, normal, dirt, trim, and edge variation.
Origins, pivots, and root empties support scene placement, repeated props, and authored collision helpers.
Foliage and alpha-card assets include clean cutout masks and do not rely on default textures.
visual_review_checks: Render Blender previews from ground, drone-height, and three-quarter angles before export.
After import, inspect the asset in S&Box lighting with material remaps, collision helpers, and scale visible.
For foliage or cards, review color texture, translucency/cutout mask, and checkerboard contact sheet.
acceptance_checks: Collision expectations are documented separately from visual mesh export.
Repeated props have stable names and avoid giant bounds.
Blockout dev-box collider sync remains a separate workflow.
